expectations.text: Configures AWS CLI profile with correct R2 endpoint format (https://abc123def456.r2.cloudflarestorage.com)
expectations.evidence: The response clearly documents AWS CLI configuration in section 1.2 with the exact endpoint format https://abc123def456.r2.cloudflarestorage.com. Multiple examples show the correct endpoint URL usage, including in the endpoint-url flag throughout Part 1 (lines 100-186).

expectations.text: Sets region to 'auto' for R2 configuration
expectations.evidence: Section 1.2 explicitly states 'The region should be set to `auto` for Cloudflare R2.' (line 71). The configuration example shows 'Default region name [None]: auto' (line 67), and the resulting ~/.aws/config file shows 'region = auto' (line 89).

expectations.text: Configures rclone with provider=Cloudflare and the correct endpoint
expectations.evidence: Section 2.2 Option A shows the rclone configuration with 'provider = Cloudflare' (line 237) and 'endpoint = https://abc123def456.r2.cloudflarestorage.com' (line 240). The interactive configuration instructions also specify choosing 'Cloudflare' as the provider (line 257).

expectations.text: Shows how to create the 'media-assets' bucket
expectations.evidence: Part 4 (Troubleshooting) section on 'NoSuchBucket' error (lines 421-427) shows the exact command to create the media-assets bucket: 'aws s3 mb s3://media-assets --endpoint-url https://abc123def456.r2.cloudflarestorage.com --profile r2'. The Quick Reference table also includes bucket creation commands for both AWS CLI and rclone (line 490).

expectations.text: Shows how to upload a test file and verify the connection
expectations.evidence: Part 3 is entirely dedicated to testing. Section 3.1 creates a test file (line 332). Section 3.2 shows upload with AWS CLI (lines 336-341) and verification by listing (lines 350-355). Section 3.3 shows rclone upload (lines 377-379) and verification (lines 382-385). Additionally, lines 363-371 show downloading and comparing with diff to verify the round-trip.
